name: Peña Saganta PR-CV 314 Loop
name_original: Peña Saganta (PR-CV 314) — Ascenso y rodeo desde Espadilla
description: Hike the moderate 6.5-mile Peña Saganta PR-CV 314 Loop for panoramic views from Peña Saganta Summit and Espadilla Castle.
region_intro: Hiking around Espadilla offers a diverse landscape characterized by hills, valleys, and areas with unique cultural features. The region includes varied terrain, from natural springs and forested paths to routes exploring local art installations. These trails provide opportunities to experience both natural beauty and human creativity. Best hiking trails around Espadilla The most popular hiking route is Peña Saganta PR-CV 314 Loop, a 6.4 miles (10.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 15 minutes to complete. This moderate route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is Urban art of Fanzara – Fanzara Street Art Murals loop from Fanzara, a moderate 7.5 miles (12.1 km) path. This trail explores a village known for its street art murals, combining cultural interest with outdoor activity. Local hikers also love the El Pozo Negro loop from Fuentes de Ayódar, a 3.1 miles (5.0 km) trail leading through natural landscapes, often completed in about 1 hour 23 minutes. Hiking around Espadilla is defined by hills, natural springs, and cultural points of interest. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Espadilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 270 reviews. More than 1600 hikers have used komoot to explore Espadilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Espadilla?
A: Espadilla offers a wide variety of hiking opportunities, with over 100 routes recorded on komoot. These trails cater to different preferences, ranging from easy walks to more challenging ascents through hills and valleys.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Espadilla?
A: The hiking trails around Espadilla feature diverse terrain, including hills, valleys, and forested paths. You'll also find natural springs and areas with unique cultural features, such as villages known for their street art installations.

Q: Are there any easy hikes suitable for beginners in Espadilla?
A: Yes, Espadilla has several easy hikes. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31433910" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Pozo Negro loop from Fuentes de Ayódar&lt;/a&gt;, which is 3.1 miles (5.0 km) long and typically takes about 1 hour 23 minutes to complete. Another easy route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14713016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Faces Art Installation – Urban art of Fanzara loop from Fanzara&lt;/a&gt;, covering 4.0 miles (6.5 km).

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Espadilla?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 270 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, from natural landscapes to routes exploring local art, and the options available for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any circular hikes in Espadilla?
A: Yes, many routes in Espadilla are circular. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e877362412/pena-saganta-pr-cv-314-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Saganta PR-CV 314 Loop&lt;/a&gt;, a 6.4-mile (10.4 km) moderate trail, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10378954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urban art of Fanzara – Fanzara Street Art Murals loop from Fanzara&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.5 miles (12.1 km).

Q: What landmarks or natural features can I see along the hiking trails?
A: Espadilla's trails offer a mix of natural and cultural points of interest. You can explore natural springs, forested paths, and unique cultural features like the street art murals in Fanzara. Notable natural attractions nearby include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2931138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de la Novia of Cirat&lt;/a&gt; waterfall and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3932056" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Órganos de Benitandús&lt;/a&gt; natural monument.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature cultural attractions?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10378954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urban art of Fanzara – Fanzara Street Art Murals loop from Fanzara&lt;/a&gt; is a prime example, taking you through a village renowned for its vibrant street art. Another related route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14713016" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Stone Faces Art Installation – Urban art of Fanzara loop from Fanzara&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or lakes to visit near Espadilla?
A: Yes, you can find beautiful natural water features near Espadilla. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2931138" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Salto de la Novia of Cirat&lt;/a&gt; is a notable waterfall, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5174600" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sichar Reservoir&lt;/a&gt; offers a scenic lake environment.

Q: What is the difficulty level of hikes in Espadilla?
A: Espadilla offers hikes for all levels. Out of over 100 routes, 19 are considered easy, 73 are moderate, and 10 are difficult. This ensures options for casual walkers and experienced hikers alike.

Q: Can I find historical sites or castles in the Espadilla area?
A: Yes, the region around Espadilla has historical points of interest. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1073196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Onda Castle&lt;/a&gt;, a significant historical landmark, and explore the historical site of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4092160" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jinquer&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the longest moderate hike in Espadilla?
A: Among the moderate routes, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10378954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urban art of Fanzara – Fanzara Street Art Murals loop from Fanzara&lt;/a&gt; is one of the longer options, spanning 7.5 miles (12.1 km) and typically taking around 3 hours 24 minutes to complete.
tips: At 726 meters above sea level, the top of Peña Saganta offers wonderful views. It is reached by a path without too much technical difficulty, although it is advisable to be well prepared, bring plenty of water, suitable footwear and hiking sticks.

faqs.question: Where does the Peña Saganta PR-CV 314 Loop trail start, and is there parking?
faqs.answer: The trail is a circular route that typically starts and ends in the village of Espadilla. While specific parking details aren't provided, it's common for trails starting in villages to have designated parking areas nearby or street parking available. It's advisable to check local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: Is the Peña Saganta PR-CV 314 Loop suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as moderate difficulty. While initial sections might be easier, the difficulty increases progressively with steep ascents, rocky paths, and an exposed section known as the "senda del túnel." It's not recommended for those unaccustomed to mountain hiking or with vertigo. Hikers should have a good level of fitness and experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the Peña Saganta PR-CV 314 Loop?
faqs.answer: The terrain is diverse and can be demanding. You'll encounter steep trails, rocky sections, and parts of a large Jurassic limestone massif with deep ravines. Much of the route follows a well-marked path ("senda"), but some sections, particularly around the castle and the "senda del túnel," can be vertical and exposed.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Peña Saganta PR-CV 314 Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail offers stunning panoramic views. Key highlights include the Peña Saganta Summit, which provides expansive vistas towards the sea, La Plana, and the Sierra de Espadán. You'll also pass the ruins of Espadilla Castle, an ancient Islamic farmhouse offering strategic viewpoints over the Mijares River. The route begins near a botanical garden, allowing for observation of various plant species.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The trail can be hiked year-round, but spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant conditions with milder temperatures. During summer, be prepared for potential lack of shade and high temperatures, making an early start and sufficient water crucial. Winter hiking is possible, but check for specific conditions, especially regarding ice or snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Is the Peña Saganta PR-CV 314 Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park generally allows dogs, it's crucial to keep them on a leash, especially given the challenging terrain, rocky sections, and potential exposure on parts of this specific trail. Ensure your dog is accustomed to strenuous hikes and bring plenty of water for them.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park?
faqs.answer: Typically, there are no entrance fees or permits required for hiking within the Sierra de Espadán Natural Park. However, it's always good practice to check the official park website or local information centers for any updated regulations or specific requirements before your visit.

faqs.question: Are there reliable water sources along the trail?
faqs.answer: Natural water sources like the Fuente de la Piqueta and the Fuente del Castillo are mentioned in the area, but they are not always reliable. It is strongly advised to carry sufficient water for the entire duration of your hike, especially during warmer months.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect other well-known paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route utilizes sections of several other paths. While primarily following the Saganta trail, it also passes through parts of Espadán - Mijares, Camino a la Cueva Negra, Camino de Espadilla a Ayódar, Camino de la Piqueta, and Camino de la Solana.

faqs.question: What makes the 'senda del túnel' section particularly challenging?
faqs.answer: The "senda del túnel" is described as the most complicated and exposed part of the trail. It involves vertical sections and may require caution due to its nature. It's not recommended for those with vertigo or limited mountain hiking experience, and a flashlight might be useful for this segment.

faqs.question: What kind of wildlife and plants can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail traverses diverse ecosystems, including areas with karst erosion. You might observe various flora such as Arbutus unedo (strawberry tree), Juniperus oxycedrus (prickly juniper), and Quercus ilex subsp. ballota (holm oak). While specific wildlife sightings aren't guaranteed, the Sierra de Espadán is home to various birds and small mammals.
